constraints: For an array AAA of integers, and KKK a parameter, where 1竕､Ai竕､K1 \le A_i \le K1竕､Ai窶銀王K, we define f(A,K)f(A, K)f(A,K) to be the maximum value of LLL such that a sequence BBB exists with the following conditions:
竏｣B竏｣=L|B| = L竏｣B竏｣=L
1竕､Bi竕､K1 \le B_i \le K1竕､Bi窶銀王K
LCS(A,B)竕､1LCS(A, B) \le 1LCS(A,B)竕､1, where LCS(A,B)LCS(A, B)LCS(A,B) represents the longest common subsequence of AAA and BBB.
For certain sequences, there is no maximum value of LLL, and we define f(A,K)=0f(A, K) = 0f(A,K)=0 in such cases.

For example, f([1,1],1)=1f([1, 1], 1) = 1f([1,1],1)=1 because we can choose B=[1]B = [1]B=[1], and f([1,1],2)=0f([1, 1], 2) = 0f([1,1],2)=0 because infinitely large sequences exist (and hence there is no maximum value of LLL).
You are given integers NNN and KKK.
Count the number of arrays satisfying the following condition, modulo 998244353998244353998244353:
竏｣A竏｣=N|A| = N竏｣A竏｣=N
1竕､Ai竕､K1 \le A_i \le K1竕､Ai窶銀王K
Input Format
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers NNN and KKK.
Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of sequences satisfying the conditions modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10410^4104.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The sequence [1][1][1] should be counted as f([1],1)=0f([1], 1) = 0f([1],1)=0.
Test Case 2 : There are no valid sequences. The only sequence satisfying the first 222 conditions is [1,1,1][1, 1, 1][1,1,1] but f([1,1,1],1)=1f([1, 1, 1], 1) = 1f([1,1,1],1)=1.
